name: napari-svg

metadata.summary: A plugin for writing svg files with napari

documentation:
# napari-svg

[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/napari-svg.svg?color=green)](https://github.com/napari/napari-svg/raw/master/LICENSE)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/napari-svg.svg?color=green)](https://pypi.org/project/napari-svg)
[![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/napari-svg.svg?color=green)](https://python.org)
[![tests](https://github.com/napari/napari-svg/workflows/tests/badge.svg)](https://github.com/napari/napari-svg/actions)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/napari/napari-svg/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/napari/napari-svg)

A plugin for writing svg files with napari

----------------------------------

This [napari] plugin was generated with [Cookiecutter] using with [@napari]'s [cookiecutter-napari-plugin] template.

<!--
Don't miss the full getting started guide to set up your new package:
https://github.com/napari/cookiecutter-napari-plugin#getting-started

and review the napari docs for plugin developers:
https://napari.org/docs/plugins/index.html
-->

## Installation

You can install `napari-svg` via [pip]:

    pip install napari-svg

## Contributing

Contributions are very welcome. Tests can be run with [tox], please ensure
the coverage at least stays the same before you submit a pull request.

## License

Distributed under the terms of the [BSD-3] license,
"napari-svg" is free and open source software

## Issues

If you encounter any problems, please [file an issue] along with a detailed description.

functions.name: napari_svg.xml_to_svg.xml_to_svg
documentation:
Convert a list of xml into an SVG string.

Parameters
----------
xml_list : list of xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    List of a xml elements in the svg specification.
extrema : array (2, 2)
    Extrema of data, specified as a minumum then maximum of the (x, y)
    coordinates. Used to specify the view box of SVG canvas.

Returns
-------
svg : string
    SVG representation of the layer.

modules.name: napari_svg._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: napari_svg._shape_to_xml.ellipse_to_xml
documentation:
Generates an xml element for an ellipse.

Only two dimensional ellipses are supported.

Parameters
----------
data : (4, 2) array
    Ellipse vertices. Only two dimensional ellipses data are supported.
svg_props : dict
    svg properties for a shape.

Returns
-------
element : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    xml element defining an ellipse.

functions.name: napari_svg._shape_to_xml.line_to_xml
documentation:
Generates an xml element for a line.

Only two dimensional lines are supported.

Parameters
----------
data : (2, 2) array
    Line vertices. Only two dimensional lines data are supported.

Returns
-------
element : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    xml element defining a line.

Returns
-------
element : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element

svg_props : dict

functions.name: napari_svg._shape_to_xml.rectangle_to_xml
documentation:
Generates an xml element for a rectangle.

Only two dimensional rectangles are supported.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.layer_transforms_to_xml_string
documentation:
Get the xml representation\[1\]\_\[2\]\_ of the layer transforms.

# Parameters

meta

: The metadata from the layer.

# Returns

tf_list

: The transformation list represented as a string.

# References

[^footnote-1]: <https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/SVG/Attribute/transform>

[^footnote-2]: <https://www.w3.org/TR/css-transforms-1/>

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.make_linear_matrix_and_offset
documentation: Make a transformation matrix from the layer metadata.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.extrema_coords
documentation: Compute the extrema of a set of coordinates after transforms in meta.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.extrema_image
documentation: Compute the extrema of an image layer, accounting for transforms.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.image_to_xml
documentation:
Generates a xml data for an image.

Only two dimensional data (including rgb, and multiscale) is supported. For
multiscale data the lowest resolution is used.

The xml data is a list with a single xml element that defines the
currently viewed image as a png according to the svg specification.

Parameters
----------
data : array or list of array
    Image data. Only two dimensional data (including rgb, and multiscale) is
    supported. For multiscale data the lowest resolution is used.
meta : dict
    Image metadata.

Returns
-------
layer_xml : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    Single xml element specifying the image as a png according to
    the svg specification.
extrema : array (2, 2)
    Extrema of data, specified as a minumum then maximum of the (x, y)
    coordinates.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.extrema_points
documentation: Compute the extrema of points, taking transformations into account.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.points_to_xml
documentation:
Generates a xml data for points.

Only two dimensional points data is supported. Z ordering of the points
will be taken into account. Each point is represented by a circle. Support
for other symbols is not yet implemented.

Note: any shear or anisotropic scaling value will be applied to the
points, so the markers themselves will be transformed and not perfect
circles anymore.

Parameters
----------
data : array
    Points data. Only two dimensional points data is supported.
meta : dict
    Points metadata.

Returns
-------
layer_xml : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    XML group element containing each point according to the
    svg specification.
extrema : array (2, 2)
    Extrema of data, specified as a minumum then maximum of the (x, y)
    coordinates.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.extrema_shapes
documentation: Compute the extrema of shapes, taking transformations into account.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.shapes_to_xml
documentation:
Generates a xml data for shapes.

Only two dimensional shapes data is supported. Z ordering of the shapes
will be taken into account.

Parameters
----------
data : list of array
    Shapes data. Only two dimensional shapes data is supported.
meta : dict
    Shapes metadata.

Returns
-------
layer_xml : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    XML group element containing each shape according to the
    svg specification
extrema : array (2, 2)
    Extrema of data, specified as a minumum then maximum of the (x, y)
    coordinates.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.extrema_vectors
documentation: Compute the extrema of vectors, taking projections into account.

functions.name: napari_svg.layer_to_xml.vectors_to_xml
documentation:
Generates a xml data for vectors.

Only two dimensional vectors data is supported. Each vector is represented
by a line.

Parameters
----------
data : array
    Vectors data. Only two dimensional vectors data is supported.
meta : dict
    Points metadata.

Returns
-------
layer_xml : xml.etree.ElementTree.Element
    XML group element containing each vector as a line according to the
    svg specification
extrema : array (2, 2)
    Extrema of data, specified as a minumum then maximum of the (x, y)
    coordinates.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.napari_get_writer
documentation:
Write layer data to an svg.

Only two dimensional data is supported.

# Parameters

path

: Path to file, directory, or resource (like a URL).

layer_types

: List of layer types that will be provided to the writer. Layer types
  must be contained in the list of currently supported layers.

# Returns

callable

: function that accepts the path and a list of layer_data (where
  layer_data is `(data, meta, layer_type)`) and writes each layer.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.writer
documentation:
Write layer data to an svg.

# Parameters

path

: path to file/directory

layer_data

: List of layer_data, where layer_data is `(data, meta, layer_type)`.

# Returns

path

: If data is successfully written, return the `path` that was written.
  Otherwise, if nothing was done, return `None`.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.napari_write_image
documentation:
Write image data to an svg.

Only two dimensional image data is supported (including rgb, and multiscale).
For multiscale data the lowest resolution is used.

# Parameters

path

: Path to file, directory, or resource (like a URL).

data

: Image data. Only two dimensional data (including rgb, and multiscale)
  is supported. For multiscale data the lowest resolution is used.

meta

: Image metadata.

# Returns

path

: If data is successfully written, return the `path` that was written.
  Otherwise, if nothing was done, return `None`.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.napari_write_labels
documentation:
Write labels data to an svg.

Only two dimensional labels data is supported (including multiscale).
For multiscale data the lowest resolution is used.

# Parameters

path

: Path to file, directory, or resource (like a URL).

data

: Labels data. Only two dimensional data (including multiscale) is supported.
  For multiscale data the lowest resolution is used.

meta

: Labels metadata.

# Returns

path

: If data is successfully written, return the `path` that was written.
  Otherwise, if nothing was done, return `None`.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.napari_write_points
documentation:
Write points data to an svg.

Only two dimensional points data is supported. Z ordering of the points
will be taken into account. Each point is represented by a circle. Support
for other symbols is not yet implemented.

# Parameters

path

: Path to file, directory, or resource (like a URL).

data

: Points data. Only two dimensional data is supported.

meta

: Points metadata.

# Returns

path

: If data is successfully written, return the `path` that was written.
  Otherwise, if nothing was done, return `None`.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.napari_write_shapes
documentation:
Write shapes data to an svg.

Only two dimensional shapes data is supported. Z ordering of the shapes

# Parameters

path

: Path to file, directory, or resource (like a URL).

data

: Shapes data. Only two dimensional data is supported.

meta

: Shapes metadata.

# Returns

path

: If data is successfully written, return the `path` that was written.
  Otherwise, if nothing was done, return `None`.

functions.name: napari_svg.hook_implementations.napari_write_vectors
documentation:
Write vectors data to an svg.

Only two dimensional vectors data is supported. Each vector is represented
by a line.

# Parameters

path

: Path to file, directory, or resource (like a URL).

data

: Vectors data. Only two dimensional data is supported.

meta

: Vectors metadata.

# Returns

path

: If data is successfully written, return the `path` that was written.
  Otherwise, if nothing was done, return `None`.
